BodySniff.description: Defines the configuration for identifying/sniffing the request body.

enabled.description: Indicates whether body capturing is enabled

template.description: The template string used to match or capture the body. Supports template variable declaration using `{{variable}}` syntax. If parameter name is suffixed with `_GRD`, greedy matching with value is used otherwise lazy.

InjectedRequestSpec.description: Specific marker interface for injected request specifications.

bodySniff.description: Identifies and captures the request body if enabled

method.description: The HTTP method used for the request. Defaults to 'GET'.

multiple.description: Whether request matching this spec is allowed to appear multiple times in list of proofs. Defaults to true.

required.description: Whether request matching this spec is required and always expected in list of proofs Defaults to true.

responseMatches.description: Required matching configurations for the HTTP response. Atleast one response match must be provided.

responseRedactions.description: Redaction rules applied to the HTTP response before passing to attestors

templateParams.description: Template parameter variables for the request spec that should be replaced with real values during dynamic request spec construction.

url.description: The URL or generic path of the HTTP request. When `urlType` is 'TEMPLATE', supports template variable declaration using `{{variable}}` syntax. If parameter name is suffixed with `_GRD`, greedy matching with value is used otherwise lazy.

urlType.description: Type or representation of the URL. If not provided, defaults to 'TEMPLATE' if url includes `{{` and 'CONSTANT' otherwise.

InterceptorRequestSpec.description: Specific marker interface for intercepted request specifications.

bodySniff.description: Identifies and captures the request body if enabled

method.description: The HTTP method used for the request. Defaults to 'GET'.

responseMatches.description: Required matching configurations for the HTTP response. Atleast one response match must be provided.

responseRedactions.description: Redaction rules applied to the HTTP response before passing to attestors

url.description: The URL or generic path of the HTTP request. When `urlType` is 'TEMPLATE', supports template variable declaration using `{{variable}}` syntax. If parameter name is suffixed with `_GRD`, greedy matching with value is used otherwise lazy.

urlType.description: Type or representation of the URL. If not provided, defaults to 'TEMPLATE' if url includes `{{` and 'CONSTANT' otherwise.

ResponseMatchSpec.description: Specifies a rule to match against a string in response to validate proof content.

invert.description: If true, the match condition is reversed. Defaults to false.

isOptional.description: If true, the match condition is optional and won't fail if absent. Defaults to false.

type.description: The matching mechanism to use value as against response. Typically regex or simple string containment.

value.description: The pattern or value to look for in the response. When `type` is 'contains', supports template variable declaration using `{{variable}}` syntax (if parameter name is suffixed with `_GRD`, greedy matching with value is used otherwise lazy). When `type` is 'regex', can be used to extract variables using regex named groups.

ResponseRedactionSpec.description: Specifies redaction rules for obscuring sensitive parts of the response.

hash.description: Optional hashing method applied to the redacted content (e.g., 'oprf'). Oprf will only work for named regex groups.

jsonPath.description: JSON path for locating the value to redact

regex.description: RegEx applied to correctly parse and extract/redact value. Can also be used to extract fields (parameters) using named regex groups.
